sourceText: This warns that even minor errors or inaccuracies in the initial stages can ultimately lead to much larger inaccuracies in the outcome.
Ibn Sina faced periods of exile and imprisonment due to political instability and power struggles. His great fame also made him a target of envy and opposition.

Despite challenging circumstances, Ibn Sina maintained his passion for scholarship. He produced numerous masterpieces through constant research and writing, and his exceptional medical skills and knowledge earned him respect, becoming a driving force to overcome adversity.
Knowledge is acquired, wisdom is practiced.
This means that wisdom is attained not just by acquiring knowledge, but by applying and practicing that knowledge in life.
The surest knowledge comes from experience.
This emphasizes that while theoretical learning is important, the knowledge gained through practical experience is the most certain and profound.
The human body is a microcosm of the universe, and the soul is connected to the cosmos.
This reflects the exploration of the mystery of human existence and its connection to the universe, highlighting the interconnectedness between the human body and the cosmos.
Inefficiency of early steam engines, lack of business capital, patent disputes, and conflicts with business partners.

Increased efficiency with the invention of the separate condenser, business expansion and secured funding through collaboration with Matthew Boulton, technological innovation through persistent research and experimentation.
I became rich by doing nothing but my business.
Focusing on one's core competencies and consistent effort is the surest path to success.
